Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 (Read Email): Search emails for Heartland-related correspondence using search_emails with a query like "Heartland" or "dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" (or similar). The agent discovers the formal dispute chain: Marcus sent a formal dispute letter (email_id: email_marcus_dispute_heartland_q1) to Jake Loomis at jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com disputing invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 for 8 moves, only 5 valid Marchetti and Osei were cancelled, Venkatesh was completed by Swift not Heartland), requesting a corrected invoice of $8,000 for 5 moves. Critically, the agent must also discover Jake Loomis's counter-proposal reply (email_id: email_heartland_counter_proposal, subject: "Re: Formal Dispute Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 MoveOps Inc."), which is unread (is_read: false). In this reply, Heartland accepts the Marchetti ($1,600 credit) and Osei ($1,600 credit) cancellations but disputes the Venkatesh charge, claiming their crews performed origin-side packing and local pickup in Houston on March 3 before the long-haul was transferred to Swift. Heartland's revised position is a corrected invoice of $9,600 for 6 service events. Jake also offers a conditional deal: settle Q1 at $9,600 and commit to a minimum volume guarantee of 8 moves in Q3, and Heartland will hold the rate increase at 12% instead of 20% ($1,792 vs $1,920 per move). This offer expires May 2.
OE 2 (Read Email): Search emails for Swift pricing changes using search_emails with a query like "Swift" or "pricing update" or "DOT surcharge" or "rate adjustment" (or similar). The agent discovers: (1) Greg Pallone's email to Chloe (email_id: email_email_9a6a5e8dc032, subject: "Pricing Update Effective Immediately DOT Documentation Surcharge") announcing a 15% base rate increase on new bookings plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per affected shipment; (2) Jake Loomis's formal rate notice to Marcus (email_id: email_email_aa260e7b26e7, subject: "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review") announcing a 20% rate increase effective July 1, 2026, tied to the unresolved Q1 dispute.
OE 3 (Read Email): Search emails for Chloe's backup vendor outreach using search_emails with a query like "Lakeline" or "backup" or "Q3 capacity" or "Sandra Walsh" (or similar). The agent discovers three emails in this thread: (1) Chloe's initial exploratory email to Sandra Walsh at Lakeline Logistics (email_id: email_email_c43077978cc3); (2) Sandra's first response with original coverage area Midwest and Southeast, approximately 10% below Heartland rates on covered lanes, not a national replacement, overflow model (email_id: email_email_4c8116551604); (3) Sandra's follow-up with expanded coverage (email_id: email_lakeline_expanded_coverage, subject: "Re: Exploratory question on Q3 corporate relocation capacity coverage update"), reporting a new subcontractor partnership with Front Range Moving out of Denver extending coverage into the Mountain West corridor (Colorado, Utah, Arizona, New Mexico). Key constraints: max 2 concurrent moves via Front Range, 30-day lead time for special handling, 14 days for standard household moves. Still cannot cover West Coast (Seattle, Portland, SF, LA) or Northeast metro (Boston, New York). Front Range partnership is a 6-month trial through October.
OE 4 (Read Slack): Search Slack for vendor margin analysis using conversations_search_messages with a query like "margin floor" or "vendor rate" or "below floor" (or similar), or retrieve channel C005 (#finance) history using conversations_history with channel_id "C005". The agent discovers Alejandro Fuentes's margin analysis post (user_id: moveops_alejandro_fuentes, ts: 1777084800.000000, channel: C005) identifying accounts that drop below the 25% margin floor if both vendor rate increases are absorbed:
- BrightLoop Analytics: current 27%, drops to approximately 19% 3 Swift moves + at least 1 Heartland in May expansion batch, plus $500 DOT surcharge on Priya Naidu's ADA shipment
- Mosaic Robotics: current 24%, already below floor before increases Keiko Tanaka laser shipment already invoiced with the $500 DOT documentation surcharge (unbudgeted, not passed to client), plus $90K prototype damage liability
- Palmetto Community Foundation: 5% nonprofit margin, any increase pushes to negative territory ($3,200 contract)
- At risk but above floor: NorthWind (28%→~25.5%), Vectral (32%→~27%), GreenStack (30%→~25%)
- Can absorb: Canopy Health (35%+), StormCloud (34%), Terraform (standard pricing)
- Data caveat: Heartland Q3 rates modeled at both 12% and 20%; the 12% scenario keeps NorthWind and Vectral above floor, the 20% scenario pushes NorthWind below
Alejandro also flags two reconciliation issues: INV-SW-2026-0419 for Keiko Tanaka shows $500 DOT surcharge but Airtable still lists original pre-surcharge vendor cost; INV-SW-2026-0422 for Daniel Reeves has a premium scheduling line item that doesn't match coordinator notes.
OE 5 (Read Slack): Search Slack for earlier vendor strategy discussions using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on channel C005 (#finance) with queries like "Heartland" or "payment hold" or "vendor" (or similar). The agent discovers: (1) Marcus's vendor payment hold announcement (ts: 1774483336) $12,800 billed vs $8,000 verified, $4,800 overcharge, formal dispute to Jake Loomis; (2) Hana's reconciliation tracker (ts: 1774484176) VRECON-2026-Q1-HM tracking details; (3) Alejandro's earlier Q3 vendor-cost exposure post (ts: 1776280500) Heartland +20% Q3, Swift +15% + $500 DOT, data gaps preventing margin forecast; (4) Marcus's memo inputs request (ts: 1777119480) delegating Heartland/Swift/Lakeline/Alejandro data gaps to Chloe, Hana, Alejandro, David with 1 PM PT deadline.
OE 6 (Read Email): Search emails for Elena's vendor strategy memo directive using search_emails with a query like "vendor strategy memo" or "Wednesday" or "memo due" (or similar). The agent discovers Elena's email to Marcus (email_id: email_email_387fcebf3efd, subject: "Vendor strategy memo due Wednesday") demanding the memo cover negotiate vs replace vs split volume, per-account margin impact, and specifically referencing Heartland notice, Swift surcharges, Hana's work, Alejandro's data, and Chloe's Lakeline conversation. The agent also discovers Marcus's delegation email (email_id: email_email_24e75fd4cfe1, subject: "Inputs required today for Wednesday vendor strategy memo") to Chloe, Hana, Alejandro, and David, CC Elena.
OE 7 (Read Linear): Search Linear issues for the vendor strategy memo ticket using linear_list_issues with a query like "vendor strategy memo" or "CEO memo" or "Heartland Swift" (or similar). The agent discovers issue_d21d94ce9b36 titled "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options," assigned to moveops_marcus_thorne, due 2026-04-29, priority 1 (P1), team_operations, with labels including executive, finance, vendor-strategy, q3-planning. The agent may also discover related issues: linear_issue_3cd312558d78 ("Create consolidated client and vendor risk grid for CEO memo") and linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 ("Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset").
OE 8 (Read Linear): Retrieve comments on the memo-related Linear issues using linear_list_comments with issueId for issue_d21d94ce9b36 or linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170. The agent discovers comments from multiple stakeholders showing the current state of inputs: Elena's directive (three paths: negotiate, replace, split volume), Marcus's acknowledgment of data gaps, Alejandro's challenges with three-system reconciliation (Airtable/QB/CRM), David's CE input on pass-through concerns, Mina's notes on Vectral and BrightLoop Swift exposure, and Emeka's Mosaic and Heartland Q3 concerns. This reveals that inputs are scattered and no consolidated draft exists yet.
OE 9 (Read CRM): Search CRM for client deals that are at risk from vendor cost increases using crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies for the affected clients. The agent discovers deals for: BrightLoop (deal_brightloop_q2: $7,000 qualifiedtobuy; deal_brightloop_apr2026: $11,350 closedwon), Mosaic (deal_mosaic_q2: $4,500; deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk: $2,700 in-progress with prototype damage; deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026: $2,770 in-progress), Palmetto (crm_deal_7ec892bc6085: $2,400), NorthWind (deal_northwind_denver_expansion: $14,700), Vectral (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk: at-risk renewal negotiation), and GreenStack (deal_greenstack_q2: $13,500; deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes: $9,440). These deals provide context for which client relationships are exposed to vendor cost pass-through risk.
OE 10 (Read QuickBooks): Search QuickBooks bills for Heartland and Swift vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills with vendor criteria. The agent discovers: bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042, TotalAmt: $12,800, Balance: $12,800 still on hold); bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (same invoice structure with lines 6-8 marked DISPUTED for Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh); Swift bills including bill_swift_tran ($2,885), bill_swift_venkatesh ($4,450), bill_swift_kovac ($3,675); and bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 contingent liability accrual against vendor_heartland). This confirms the financial exposure: $12,800 Heartland payment on hold, $11,010 in outstanding Swift bills, and $90,000 damage accrual.
OE 11 (Read QuickBooks): Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices for Mosaic and other affected client invoices. The agent discovers inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (DocNumber: INV-2026-0411, TotalAmt: $9,010) which includes an $800 hazmat handling surcharge line item for Keiko Tanaka's laser shipment via Swift. This confirms that the new Swift DOT-related pricing has already been incurred on an active shipment. Cross-referencing with Alejandro's Slack analysis, the $500 DOT documentation surcharge component was not budgeted in the original deal pricing and has not been passed to the client.
OE 12 (Read Airtable): Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table "Relocations" (tblRelocations01), looking for Keiko Tanaka or Mosaic records. The agent discovers that the Keiko Tanaka record (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) lists the Swift move with DOT 49 CFR hazmat handling but the vendor cost field does not reflect the $500 DOT surcharge that has already been billed by Swift. This Airtable-to-QuickBooks discrepancy confirms that an active shipment has been hit by the new pricing without the records being updated, validating the need for a tracking ticket.
OE 13 (Read Contacts): Look up Marcus Thorne's contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Marcus Thorne" (or similar) to confirm his email address (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) before sending the gap list email. The agent also confirms Elena Rostova's email (elena.rostova@moveops.com) for the sender/CC field.
OE 14 (Write Linear): Update the Linear ticket tracking the vendor strategy memo using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_d21d94ce9b36" (or linear_update_issue on the same id). The comment/update should describe the current status: Heartland has responded to the dispute with a counter-proposal revised invoice of $9,600 for 6 moves (accepts Marchetti and Osei credits, disputes Venkatesh origin-side work), with a conditional 12% rate hold offer tied to 8-move Q3 minimum, expiring May 2; Swift's 15% base increase and $500 DOT surcharge are in effect and already impacting the Keiko Tanaka Mosaic shipment; Lakeline backup has expanded to include Mountain West via Front Range Moving (CO, UT, AZ, NM) but still cannot cover West Coast or Northeast; Alejandro's margin analysis identifies BrightLoop, Mosaic, and Palmetto below the 25% floor with NorthWind, Vectral, and GreenStack at risk; no draft memo exists yet only scattered inputs across Slack, email, and Linear comments.
OE 15 (Write CRM): Create CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement for clients where the vendor cost increases create relationship risk. The agent should create engagements for at minimum:
- BrightLoop Analytics (company_id: company_brightloop): margin drops from 27% to approximately 19% with Swift increases, below the 25% floor, during an account already in service recovery. Contact_ids should include the relevant BrightLoop CRM contact.
- Mosaic Robotics (company_id: company_mosaic): already below floor at 24%, compounded by the unbudgeted $500 DOT surcharge on Keiko Tanaka and $90K prototype damage liability making it the worst financial position in the book. Contact_ids should include the relevant Mosaic CRM contact.
- Palmetto Community Foundation: 5% nonprofit margin goes negative with any vendor increase, small dollar amount ($3,200) but precedent-setting for nonprofit pricing.
Additional at-risk clients warranting engagement notes: NorthWind Technologies, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy. Each engagement body should note the specific vendor cost impact and margin risk created by the dual rate increases.
OE 16 (Write Email): Send an email using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC elena.rostova@moveops.com. The email should list the specific gaps Marcus needs to close before Wednesday:
1. Heartland counter-proposal response Jake's revised $9,600 invoice position and the conditional 12% vs 20% rate offer, which expires May 2
2. Venkatesh dispute resolution Heartland claims origin-side packing was completed March 3, need to verify or rebut with evidence
3. Swift surcharge pass-through plan which clients absorb vs pass through the 15% increase and $500 DOT surcharge
4. Lakeline backup viability updated coverage now includes Mountain West via Front Range but still no West Coast or Northeast, need assessment of how much volume Lakeline can realistically absorb
5. Per-account margin impact reconciliation Alejandro has the analysis but flagged Airtable vs QuickBooks mismatches on Keiko Tanaka and Daniel Reeves that need resolution
6. Client communication plan for accounts below the margin floor (BrightLoop, Mosaic, Palmetto)
OE 17 (Write Slack): Post a consolidated vendor status message in channel C005 (#finance) using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C005". The message should consolidate the full vendor picture so people stop working off scattered threads:
1. Heartland dispute: counter-proposal received Marchetti and Osei credits accepted, Venkatesh charge disputed (origin-side work claim), revised invoice $9,600 for 6 moves; conditional 12% rate hold offer if 8-move Q3 minimum committed, expires May 2
2. Swift pricing: 15% base increase and $500 DOT documentation surcharge in effect; already impacting Keiko Tanaka Mosaic shipment (unbudgeted cost absorbed)
3. Lakeline backup: expanded coverage now includes Mountain West (CO, UT, AZ, NM) via Front Range Moving subcontractor; max 2 concurrent moves, 30-day lead for special handling; still no West Coast or Northeast coverage
4. Margin impact: BrightLoop (~19%), Mosaic (24% and falling), Palmetto (5%) below 25% floor; NorthWind, Vectral, GreenStack at risk depending on Heartland scenario (12% vs 20%)
5. Data gaps: Airtable/QuickBooks reconciliation needed on Keiko Tanaka DOT surcharge and Daniel Reeves premium scheduling
6. Memo status: no draft yet, inputs needed by Wednesday
OE 18 (Write Linear): Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue to track the fact that the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics shipment (and potentially Daniel Reeves / Vectral) has already been financially impacted by the new Swift pricing without proper tracking or client notification. The issue should be created on team_operations or team_relocations with a descriptive title such as "Untracked Swift DOT surcharge on Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic vendor cost reconciliation needed" (or similar). The description should note that INV-SW-2026-0419 includes the $500 DOT surcharge but the Airtable relocation record still lists the original pre-surcharge vendor cost, the surcharge was not budgeted in the Mosaic deal pricing, and the cost has been absorbed without client communication. The Daniel Reeves premium scheduling mismatch on INV-SW-2026-0422 may also be included in this ticket or created as a separate issue.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1 (Read Email): Search emails for Heartland-related correspondence using search_emails with a query like "Heartland" or "dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" (or similar). The agent discovers the formal dispute chain: Marcus sent a formal dispute letter (email_id: email_marcus_dispute_heartland_q1) to Jake Loomis at jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com disputing invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 for 8 moves, only 5 valid Marchetti and Osei were cancelled, Venkatesh was completed by Swift not Heartland), requesting a corrected invoice of $8,000 for 5 moves. Critically, the agent must also discover Jake Loomis's counter-proposal reply (email_id: email_heartland_counter_proposal, subject: "Re: Formal Dispute Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 MoveOps Inc."), which is unread (is_read: false). In this reply, Heartland accepts the Marchetti ($1,600 credit) and Osei ($1,600 credit) cancellations but disputes the Venkatesh charge, claiming their crews performed origin-side packing and local pickup in Houston on March 3 before the long-haul was transferred to Swift. Heartland's revised position is a corrected invoice of $9,600 for 6 service events. Jake also offers a conditional deal: settle Q1 at $9,600 and commit to a minimum volume guarantee of 8 moves in Q3, and Heartland will hold the rate increase at 12% instead of 20% ($1,792 vs $1,920 per move). This offer expires May 2.

The agent discovers: (1) Marcus's vendor payment hold announcement (ts: 1774483336) $12,800 billed vs $8,000 verified, $4,800 overcharge, formal dispute to Jake Loomis; (2) Hana's reconciliation tracker (ts: 1774484176) VRECON-2026-Q1-HM tracking details; (3) Alejandro's earlier Q3 vendor-cost exposure post (ts: 1776280500) Heartland +20% Q3, Swift +15% + $500 DOT, data gaps preventing margin forecast; (4) Marcus's memo inputs request (ts: 1777119480) delegating Heartland/Swift/Lakeline/Alejandro data gaps to Chloe, Hana, Alejandro, David with 1 PM PT deadline.
OE 6 (Read Email): Search emails for Elena's vendor strategy memo directive using search_emails with a query like "vendor strategy memo" or "Wednesday" or "memo due" (or similar). The agent discovers Elena's email to Marcus (email_id: email_email_387fcebf3efd, subject: "Vendor strategy memo due Wednesday") demanding the memo cover negotiate vs replace vs split volume, per-account margin impact, and specifically referencing Heartland notice, Swift surcharges, Hana's work, Alejandro's data, and Chloe's Lakeline conversation. The agent also discovers Marcus's delegation email (email_id: email_email_24e75fd4cfe1, subject: "Inputs required today for Wednesday vendor strategy memo") to Chloe, Hana, Alejandro, and David, CC Elena.
OE 7 (Read Linear): Search Linear issues for the vendor strategy memo ticket using linear_list_issues with a query like "vendor strategy memo" or "CEO memo" or "Heartland Swift" (or similar). The agent discovers issue_d21d94ce9b36 titled "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options," assigned to moveops_marcus_thorne, due 2026-04-29, priority 1 (P1), team_operations, with labels including executive, finance, vendor-strategy, q3-planning. The agent may also discover related issues: linear_issue_3cd312558d78 ("Create consolidated client and vendor risk grid for CEO memo") and linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 ("Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset").
OE 8 (Read Linear): Retrieve comments on the memo-related Linear issues using linear_list_comments with issueId for issue_d21d94ce9b36 or linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170. The agent discovers comments from multiple stakeholders showing the current state of inputs: Elena's directive (three paths: negotiate, replace, split volume), Marcus's acknowledgment of data gaps, Alejandro's challenges with three-system reconciliation (Airtable/QB/CRM), David's CE input on pass-through concerns, Mina's notes on Vectral and BrightLoop Swift exposure, and Emeka's Mosaic and Heartland Q3 concerns. This reveals that inputs are scattered and no consolidated draft exists yet.
OE 9 (Read CRM): Search CRM for client deals that are at risk from vendor cost increases using crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies for the affected clients. The agent discovers deals for: BrightLoop (deal_brightloop_q2: $7,000 qualifiedtobuy; deal_brightloop_apr2026: $11,350 closedwon), Mosaic (deal_mosaic_q2: $4,500; deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk: $2,700 in-progress with prototype damage; deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026: $2,770 in-progress), Palmetto (crm_deal_7ec892bc6085: $2,400), NorthWind (deal_northwind_denver_expansion: $14,700), Vectral (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk: at-risk renewal negotiation), and GreenStack (deal_greenstack_q2: $13,500; deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes: $9,440). These deals provide context for which client relationships are exposed to vendor cost pass-through risk.
OE 10 (Read QuickBooks): Search QuickBooks bills for Heartland and Swift vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills with vendor criteria. The agent discovers: bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042, TotalAmt: $12,800, Balance: $12,800 still on hold); bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (same invoice structure with lines 6-8 marked DISPUTED for Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh); Swift bills including bill_swift_tran ($2,885), bill_swift_venkatesh ($4,450), bill_swift_kovac ($3,675); and bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 contingent liability accrual against vendor_heartland). This confirms the financial exposure: $12,800 Heartland payment on hold, $11,010 in outstanding Swift bills, and $90,000 damage accrual.
OE 11 (Read QuickBooks): Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices for Mosaic and other affected client invoices. The agent discovers inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (DocNumber: INV-2026-0411, TotalAmt: $9,010) which includes an $800 hazmat handling surcharge line item for Keiko Tanaka's laser shipment via Swift. This confirms that the new Swift DOT-related pricing has already been incurred on an active shipment. Cross-referencing with Alejandro's Slack analysis, the $500 DOT documentation surcharge component was not budgeted in the original deal pricing and has not been passed to the client.
OE 12 (Read Airtable): Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table "Relocations" (tblRelocations01), looking for Keiko Tanaka or Mosaic records. The agent discovers that the Keiko Tanaka record (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) lists the Swift move with DOT 49 CFR hazmat handling but the vendor cost field does not reflect the $500 DOT surcharge that has already been billed by Swift. This Airtable-to-QuickBooks discrepancy confirms that an active shipment has been hit by the new pricing without the records being updated, validating the need for a tracking ticket.
OE 13 (Read Contacts): Look up Marcus Thorne's contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Marcus Thorne" (or similar) to confirm his email address (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) before sending the gap list email. The agent also confirms Elena Rostova's email (elena.rostova@moveops.com) for the sender/CC field.
OE 14 (Write Linear): Update the Linear ticket tracking the vendor strategy memo using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_d21d94ce9b36" (or linear_update_issue on the same id). The comment/update should describe the current status: Heartland has responded to the dispute with a counter-proposal revised invoice of $9,600 for 6 moves (accepts Marchetti and Osei credits, disputes Venkatesh origin-side work), with a conditional 12% rate hold offer tied to 8-move Q3 minimum, expiring May 2; Swift's 15% base increase and $500 DOT surcharge are in effect and already impacting the Keiko Tanaka Mosaic shipment; Lakeline backup has expanded to include Mountain West via Front Range Moving (CO, UT, AZ, NM) but still cannot cover West Coast or Northeast; Alejandro's margin analysis identifies BrightLoop, Mosaic, and Palmetto below the 25% floor with NorthWind, Vectral, and GreenStack at risk; no draft memo exists yet only scattered inputs across Slack, email, and Linear comments.
OE 15 (Write CRM): Create CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement for clients where the vendor cost increases create relationship risk. The agent should create engagements for at minimum:
- BrightLoop Analytics (company_id: company_brightloop): margin drops from 27% to approximately 19% with Swift increases, below the 25% floor, during an account already in service recovery. Contact_ids should include the relevant BrightLoop CRM contact.
- Mosaic Robotics (company_id: company_mosaic): already below floor at 24%, compounded by the unbudgeted $500 DOT surcharge on Keiko Tanaka and $90K prototype damage liability making it the worst financial position in the book. Contact_ids should include the relevant Mosaic CRM contact.
- Palmetto Community Foundation: 5% nonprofit margin goes negative with any vendor increase, small dollar amount ($3,200) but precedent-setting for nonprofit pricing.
Additional at-risk clients warranting engagement notes: NorthWind Technologies, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy. Each engagement body should note the specific vendor cost impact and margin risk created by the dual rate increases.
OE 16 (Write Email): Send an email using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC elena.rostova@moveops.com. The email should list the specific gaps Marcus needs to close before Wednesday:
1. Heartland counter-proposal response Jake's revised $9,600 invoice position and the conditional 12% vs 20% rate offer, which expires May 2
2. Venkatesh dispute resolution Heartland claims origin-side packing was completed March 3, need to verify or rebut with evidence
3. Swift surcharge pass-through plan which clients absorb vs pass through the 15% increase and $500 DOT surcharge
4. Lakeline backup viability updated coverage now includes Mountain West via Front Range but still no West Coast or Northeast, need assessment of how much volume Lakeline can realistically absorb
5. Per-account margin impact reconciliation Alejandro has the analysis but flagged Airtable vs QuickBooks mismatches on Keiko Tanaka and Daniel Reeves that need resolution
6. Client communication plan for accounts below the margin floor (BrightLoop, Mosaic, Palmetto)
OE 17 (Write Slack): Post a consolidated vendor status message in channel C005 (#finance) using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C005". The message should consolidate the full vendor picture so people stop working off scattered threads:
1. Heartland dispute: counter-proposal received Marchetti and Osei credits accepted, Venkatesh charge disputed (origin-side work claim), revised invoice $9,600 for 6 moves; conditional 12% rate hold offer if 8-move Q3 minimum committed, expires May 2
2. Swift pricing: 15% base increase and $500 DOT documentation surcharge in effect; already impacting Keiko Tanaka Mosaic shipment (unbudgeted cost absorbed)
3. Lakeline backup: expanded coverage now includes Mountain West (CO, UT, AZ, NM) via Front Range Moving subcontractor; max 2 concurrent moves, 30-day lead for special handling; still no West Coast or Northeast coverage
4. Margin impact: BrightLoop (~19%), Mosaic (24% and falling), Palmetto (5%) below 25% floor; NorthWind, Vectral, GreenStack at risk depending on Heartland scenario (12% vs 20%)
5. Data gaps: Airtable/QuickBooks reconciliation needed on Keiko Tanaka DOT surcharge and Daniel Reeves premium scheduling
6. Memo status: no draft yet, inputs needed by Wednesday
OE 18 (Write Linear): Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue to track the fact that the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics shipment (and potentially Daniel Reeves / Vectral) has already been financially impacted by the new Swift pricing without proper tracking or client notification. The issue should be created on team_operations or team_relocations with a descriptive title such as "Untracked Swift DOT surcharge on Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic vendor cost reconciliation needed" (or similar). The description should note that INV-SW-2026-0419 includes the $500 DOT surcharge but the Airtable relocation record still lists the original pre-surcharge vendor cost, the surcharge was not budgeted in the Mosaic deal pricing, and the cost has been absorbed without client communication. The Daniel Reeves premium scheduling mismatch on INV-SW-2026-0422 may also be included in this ticket or created as a separate issue.

outcome

32 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_list_comments) to look up the vendor strategy memo ticket and its current status.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `linear_list_issues` with query 'vendor strategy memo' early in the trajectory, which correctly found issue #318 ('Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options').
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'vendor strategy memo' (and also 'Heartland' and 'Swift') to look up the relevant ticket before updating it. This satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows linear_list_issues calls with queries 'vendor strategy memo', 'Heartland', and 'vendor margin dataset', plus a linear_list_comments call on issue_id 'linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36'. The agent properly looked up the memo ticket before updating it.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows linear_list_issues called with query: 'vendor strategy memo', returning the relevant ticket, and also called with 'Heartland' and 'vendor margin dataset'. The memo ticket was found and subsequently updated.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows linear_list_issues was called with query 'vendor strategy memo', which returned the relevant issue (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36). Also called with 'Heartland' and 'margin' queries.
✓Run #6The agent called `linear_list_issues` with query 'vendor strategy memo' to locate the tracking ticket before updating it.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #5The trajectory shows crm_search_deals calls for BrightLoop, Mosaic, NorthWind, Vectral, GreenStack, and Palmetto — all affected client accounts. Also crm_search_companies was called for Heartland and Swift.

34 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_invoices) to look up vendor bills or client invoices related to Heartland or Swift.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `quickbooks_search_bills` with `fetchAll: true`. While `quickbooks_search_vendors` errored, the `quickbooks_search_bills` call successfully retrieved QuickBooks bill data directly relevant to vendor billing.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering for 'Heartland' and 'Swift' vendor names. Although both calls returned errors, the tool was used as required by the criterion.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true) and quickbooks_search_invoices calls, directly checking QuickBooks for vendor bills and client invoices related to Heartland and Swift.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_bills called twice with vendor-specific criteria (Heartland, Swift) and once with fetchAll: true. Although the first two calls returned errors, the tool was used and the fetchAll call succeeded.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll: true was called, which returned vendor bills. Also quickbooks_search_vendors was attempted (though it initially errored, then was retried with fetchAll).
✓Run #6The agent called `quickbooks_search_bills` with `fetchAll: true` to retrieve vendor bills from the books, satisfying the requirement to check QuickBooks.
35 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) to look up relocation records for affected client moves.
tool selection
 ✗✓✓✓✗✓ 67%
✗Run #1The agent only called `airtable_list_bases` to discover available bases but never followed up with `airtable_search_records` or `airtable_list_records` to actually look up relocation records. The criterion requires looking up relocation records for affected client moves, which requires searching/listing records, not just listing bases.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_list_bases to discover the available Airtable bases (MoveOps Operations and MoveOps Relocations). This qualifies as using an Airtable tool to look up relocation records.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows airtable_list_records calls on base 'appMoveOpsOps001' for both 'Relocations' and 'Client Accounts' tables, directly searching Airtable for relocation records relevant to affected clients.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables calls attempting to access relocation records. The tables returned empty (no records found), but the agent did use Airtable tools to attempt to discover and search relocation data.
✗Run #5The trajectory shows only airtable_list_bases was called. No airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records calls were made to actually look up relocation records for affected client moves. The criterion specifically requires searching relocation records.
✓Run #6The agent called `airtable_list_bases` and `airtable_list_tables` to look up Airtable relocation records, satisfying the requirement to use an Airtable tool.

query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #6The agent called `search_emails` with queries 'Heartland' and 'dispute', both of which match the required search terms to discover the Heartland counter-proposal reply.

38 The model must pass a query related to "margin" or "vendor rate" or "margin floor" or "below floor" (or similar) when searching Slack messages (via conversations_search_messages) or retrieving #finance channel history (via conversations_history with channel C005) to discover Alejandro's margin analysis.
query construction
 ✓✗✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent called `conversations_search_messages` with `search_query: 'margin'` and `filter_in_channel: '#finance'`, which directly targets the #finance channel for margin-related content — precisely the query needed to discover Alejandro's margin analysis.
✗Run #2The agent's conversations_search_messages queries were 'Heartland', 'Swift', 'vendor strategy memo', 'backup vendor', and 'Lakeline'. None of these queries targeted 'margin', 'vendor rate', 'margin floor', or 'below floor'. No conversations_history call for C005 with a margin-related focus appears. The margin data seems to have been surfaced through the Heartland/Swift/Slack searches without a targeted margin query.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows conversations_search_messages with 'margin floor client' and 'client margin risk vendor increase', plus conversations_history on channel_id 'C005' (the finance channel). These are appropriate queries to discover Alejandro's margin analysis in the #finance channel.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows conversations_search_messages called with 'margin floor client' and 'client margin floor absorb', matching the criterion's requirement for margin-related queries to discover Alejandro's margin analysis in the finance channel.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows conversations_search_messages with search_query 'margin' was called, which would surface Alejandro's margin analysis post in #finance.
✓Run #6The agent called `conversations_search_messages` with `search_query: 'margin'` and `filter_in_channel: '#finance'`, directly matching the required query to discover Alejandro's margin analysis.
